content: @Danilo Krummrich I can't find my work-around for the inline clippy issue. Do you remember where you saw it?

I can't find it either, I think @Gary Guo did remember it, but if I'm not mistaken it simply was:

// `my_offset` is a reference.
let offset: usize = *my_offset;

mmio.write32(0x42, offset);
Hmmmm I don't see where that matches my code
If you give me a tree with steps to reproduce the failure, I can take a look
I would be good if we can also find cases where Clippy codegen differently w.r.t. rustc and report a bug upstream, too.
The fix is already upstream, just not in drm-rust-next where I need it.
I meant upstream Clippy
I wouldn't even know where to begin with that.
I think that's fine, you don't have to take care of reporting it yourself, but sharing your case does help.
Yeah, I can report to Clippy or even attempt a fix myself, however I do need a reproduction on when exactly the codegen is broken
https://github.com/ttabi/linux/commits/turing.temp/

Building with LLVM
DESCEND objtool
CALL scripts/checksyscalls.sh
INSTALL libsubcmd_headers
RUSTC L rust/core.o
EXPORTS rust/exports_core_generated.h
CLIPPY L rust/compiler_builtins.o
CLIPPY L rust/ffi.o
CLIPPY L rust/pin_init.o
CLIPPY L rust/build_error.o
CLIPPY L rust/bindings.o
CLIPPY L rust/uapi.o
EXPORTS rust/exports_bindings_generated.h
CLIPPY L rust/kernel.o
EXPORTS rust/exports_kernel_generated.h
UPD init/utsversion-tmp.h
CLIPPY [M] drivers/gpu/nova-core/nova_core.o
CC init/version.o
CLIPPY [M] drivers/net/phy/qt2025.o
AR init/built-in.a
CC rust/exports.o
AR rust/built-in.a
CLIPPY [M] drivers/gpu/drm/nova/nova.o
drivers/gpu/nova-core/nova_core.o: warning: objtool: _RNvMs_NtCs1ewLyjEZ7Le_6kernel2ioINtB4_2IoKj1000000_E7write32Csbj1oSGstQr7_9nova_core() falls through to next function _RNvMs_NtCs1ewLyjEZ7Le_6kernel3pciINtB4_7AdapterNtNtCsbj1oSGstQr7_9nova_core6driver8NovaCoreE14probe_callbackBN_()
AR built-in.a
AR vmlinux.a
LD vmlinux.o
MODPOST Module.symvers
ERROR: modpost: "rust_build_error" [drivers/gpu/nova-core/nova_core.ko] undefined!
make[2]: *** [scripts/Makefile.modpost:147: Module.symvers] Error 1
make[1]: *** [/home/ttabi/nova/Makefile:2004: modpost] Error 2
make: *** [Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
I'll take a look tomorrow

Ok, I have a v7 of my patchset that's ready to go otherwise. I believe the problem is the writes to NV_PFALCON_FALCON_IMEMC and other registers in pio_wr_slice() that take a third parameter to .write(), e.g. .write(bar, &E::ID, port);
even if 'port' is hard-coded to 0, CLIPPY can't optimize away the assertions.
No, I don't think that's it any more. I removed the 'port' from all these registers, and it still fails. It must be something else.

Gary Guo said:

I would be good if we can also find cases where Clippy codegen differently w.r.t. rustc and report a bug upstream, too.

Clippy intentionally documents that they may not generate the same code, so certain things may not be a bug -- I sent this PR to document it after asking the mantainers a few years ago:

https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/pull/8037
https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/8035

In particular, @bjorn3 explained back then this snippet I asked about indeed intentionally disables MIR optimizations which in turn also affect LLVM optimizations too:

 // FIXME: #4825; This is required, because Clippy lints that are based on MIR have to be
 // run on the unoptimized MIR. On the other hand this results in some false negatives. If
 // MIR passes can be enabled / disabled separately, we should figure out, what passes to
 // use for Clippy.
 config.opts.debugging_opts.mir_opt_level = Some(0);
That is why in the kernel we say that using CLIPPY=1 should never be used for "final" (proper/production/...) builds.
Right. Perhaps better to use clippy to check and then run rustc for binary artifact then?
Exactly, that is what we recommend.
I documented it at: https://docs.kernel.org/rust/general-information.html#extra-lints
No, I meant to run rustc once and clippy once when building with clippy=1, i.e. to run clippy as additional check
Please note that Clippy may change code generation, thus it should not be enabled while building a production kernel.

Gary Guo said:

No, I meant to run rustc once and clippy once when building with clippy=1, i.e. to run clippy as additional check

Hmm... I don't think we want to double the build time and space.
i.e. using CLIPPY=1 should generally be fine for development builds etc.
Well, I'd imagine you would soon need to run both clippy and klint for your development build once more lint is implemented :)
So far the only issue I recall with it was the missing #[inline]s for the build error, which seems fair since we have a solution, right? (i.e. we just add the inlines).

Gary Guo said:

Well, I'd imagine you would soon need to run both clippy and klint for your development build once more lint is implemented :)

Could we do both passes at once?
I don't see an easy way of doing that. Both wants to be compiler drivers

Hmm... it would be nice to get that somehow, even if it means one of the drivers taking the lead role and "chaining" the other pass(es), because ideally most kernel developers would eventually run klint most of the time.
I could take a look, but it's not going to be a priority.
Ah, yeah, no worries, I meant it in the sense of asking upstream Rust in case there could be a way to make it easier for you somehow eventually.

I think clippy disables MIR optimization is just a workaround really. We can have rustc provide needed MIR so clippy doesn't need to do this. klint specifically has a way to steal out unoptimized MIR even if rustc wanting to optimise things
If it helps, we could ask Clippy to join one of the next meetings?
If they can get rid of the "different codegen" thing, then that is great on its own too, of course.
I think this is quite compiler internal and it probably won't help to have in our usual meeting. I could go and file a MCP and change clippy and rustc impl, if needed.
As you prefer!
And thanks either way :)
I found in this case it's indeed clippy deciding that #[inline] function doesn't need inlining.
The whole write function is emitted to the output as is.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Gary Guo said:

No, I meant to run rustc once and clippy once when building with clippy=1, i.e. to run clippy as additional check

Hmm... I don't think we want to double the build time and space.

BTW the space won't be doubled. The build time is going to increase, but won't double as significant amount of time is LLVM codegen, which we can completely avoid by running clippy without generating binary artifact.

Here's the workaround @Timur Tabi

Add rustflags-y += -Coverflow-checks=n to nova-core/Makefile. This makes the function simple enough that LLVM will inline it regardless without any MIR opts.
Hmmm I'm not sure a change to the Makefile is what Danilo had in mind.
Otherwise you'd need the patch to actually land.
Yeah, please try to avoid changes like rustflags-y (especially since =y is meant to be the default).
